summary: The user discussed their project focused on organizing a community-driven Ethereum event in Mexico, emphasizing its unique aspects compared to previous events. They outlined plans for sustainability post-grant and clarified their funding request while expressing flexibility regarding the grant amount.
facts: The user is part of the Ethereum México Community Team, which aims to create a community-driven event for Web3 in Mexico. Last year, the user participated in two major blockchain events in Mexico, ETHMexico and Blockchainland, both attracting over 700 attendees. The user has previously applied for funding from ESP to host smaller events during The Merge, successfully organizing three events in three different cities with 80-100 attendees each. The user is requesting a grant of 20k USD from the Ethereum Foundation but is open to discussing different grant amounts. The user believes that the Ethereum Mexico event will significantly impact the Ethereum ecosystem in Mexico. The user plans to continue organizing community events and workshops after the grant is completed. The user aims to build long-lasting partnerships to support ongoing community-building efforts. The user recognizes the limitations of small community events compared to a larger, high-quality event like Ethereum Mexico.
